conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and he's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are responsible for paying for things like his food, clothing, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me at home, and I'm responsible for all his needs - his food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. A few more questions:

**Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? Since he's 8 years old, I assume he's in primary school - is that correct?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. He's in primary school, Year 4.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions:

**Does Alex live with anyone else besides you (for example, another parent or partner)? And if so, is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's just the two of us at home. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just one final question:

**Is Alex currently in local authority care, or has he been in hospital for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he was in local authority care for a little while, but it was only for a couple of months earlier this year. He's been back home with me since then.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. To clarify:

**Is Alex currently still in local authority care, or is he fully back living with you at home now?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's fully back living with me at home now. That period in care has ended.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Let me just confirm one more detail:

**How long has Alex been back living with you at home since the period in local authority care ended?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been back home with me for a few weeks now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements:

✓ You are responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you pay for his needs
✓ You both live in the UK (Manchester)
✓ Alex is 8 years old and in primary school (under 16)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
✓ Although Alex was in local authority care briefly earlier this year, he is now fully back home living with you

Since Alex is no longer in local authority care and is living with you full-time with you being responsible for him, you can claim Child Benefit for him.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria: (1) They are responsible for Alex - he lives with them and they pay for his needs including food, clothing, and school items; (2) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (Manchester); (3) Alex is 8 years old and in Year 4 primary school, which qualifies as he is under 16; (4) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (5) Although Alex was in local authority care earlier this year for a couple of months, he is now fully back living at home with the claimant and has been for a few weeks. Since he is no longer in local authority care and is living with the claimant who is responsible for him, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. All eligibility requirements are met: the claimant is responsible for Alex who lives with them in the UK, Alex is under 16 and in education, no one else is claiming for him, and although he was briefly in local authority care earlier this year, he has now returned home and is living with the claimant full-time.
